prose:
Method-provenance block
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor; most-electrophilic site is atom 3 (C) with f⁺ = 0.39938969 (dimensionless).
Level of theory: DFT, tier “standard” → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (PySCF backend).
Geometry provenance: user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz (14 atoms) taken as-is; single-points were run for the neutral (N), cation (N–1; charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (N+1; charge –1, multiplicity 2) on that fixed geometry (DFT//input-geometry).
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge/multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, mult 1) for the reference state; open-shell UKS for the ±1 charged species.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill calling PySCF v2.x (exact version in CLI transcript below).
Convergence & warnings: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; chemkit returned three warnings, reproduced verbatim here:
• "sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)"
• "common header present"
Integrity check verdict: trustworthy = true.

Reproducibility
CLI command (from result JSON):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/benzaldehyde.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmponig9tfe/live.json

Most-electrophilic atom: atom 3 (C, the carbonyl carbon) with f⁺ = 0.39938969 (dimensionless).
